meaning: n.领结,领巾,围巾,三角绷带
examples: They wore ordinary ties instead of the more formal high collar and cravat.
Croatia also has a place in the history of neckwear as the origin of the necktie (cravat).
The one with his arm hung up in a black cravat?
In his Bermuda shorts, orange shirt, and cravat, Jackson Irungu is getting a manicure.
Its ads replaced John Cleese, a cravat-wearing ex-Python, with the blokish Jamie Oliver, a chef for the average Brit.
